SEARCH
Package graph

Interface Agent

All Known Implementing Classes:
BinOpAgent, IncAgent, MulAgent, ParallelAgent, PlusAgent, TextMergeAgent
public interface Agent
Defines the operational contract for reactive processing nodes (agents) within the message-routing network.

An agent acts as a message consumer (Observer) subscribing to topics, executing custom processing strategies upon receiving updates, and optionally publishing results to downstream topics.

Threading and Execution Contract:

To prevent processing delays (e.g., intensive mathematical computations) from blocking the publishing threads, implementations can be decorated using graph.ParallelAgent (Active Object design pattern). This decouples the thread initiating the message publication from the thread executing the agent's actual callback(String, Message) logic.

Lifecycle States:

  • Identification (getName()): Returns a descriptive, unique string identifying the agent instance within the topology.
  • Message Arrival (callback(String, Message)): Re-actively handles messages dispatched by subscribed topics.
  • State Reinitialization (reset()): Restores the agent's internal caches and parameters back to their default structural starting values.
  • Graceful Disposal (close()): Disconnects topic subscriptions and unregisters from publishers to release execution and directory system resources.

Code Integration Example:


 Agent myAgent = new Agent() {
     private double lastValue = 0.0;
     
     @Override public String getName() { return "CustomAgent"; }
     @Override public void reset() { lastValue = 0.0; }
     @Override public void close() { // Unsubscribe if necessary }
     
     @Override
     public void callback(String topic, Message msg) {
         if (msg != null && !Double.isNaN(msg.asDouble)) {
             lastValue = msg.asDouble;
             System.out.println("Processed: " + lastValue);
         }
     }
 };
See Also:

  • TopicManagerSingleton

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    callback(String topic, Message msg)
    Reactively processes incoming messages dispatched from subscription topics.
    void
    close()
    Cleans up all topic subscriptions and unregisters publisher entries from the global directory before disposal, preventing dangling references and memory leaks.
    String
    getName()
    Retrieves the unique, descriptive name of this agent instance.
    void
    reset()
    Restores the agent's internal cache values and state variables back to their default structural starting values.

  • Method Details

    • getName

      String getName()
      Retrieves the unique, descriptive name of this agent instance.
      Returns:
      the agent's name string

    • reset

      void reset()
      Restores the agent's internal cache values and state variables back to their default structural starting values.

    • callback

      void callback(String topic, Message msg)
      Reactively processes incoming messages dispatched from subscription topics.

      Implementations must handle incoming data defensively, ignoring null values or invalid numeric payloads (Double.NaN) to ensure processing thread stability.

      Parameters:
      topic - the name of the topic that dispatched the update
      msg - the immutable message container holding the new payload value

    • close

      void close()
      Cleans up all topic subscriptions and unregisters publisher entries from the global directory before disposal, preventing dangling references and memory leaks.
